meta.name: Region gene-expression differences (Kruskal-Wallis)
meta.description: For each cell type, test which genes differ in expression across regions with the Kruskal-Wallis H-test (non-parametric, multi-sample), then plot the top region-varying genes for one cell type as a gene × region heatmap.
meta.provenance: Kruskal-Wallis H-test (Kruskal & Wallis, JASA 1952) with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR; original to this repository. Step 6 of the Xenium region-analysis workflow.

readme: Step 6 of the workflow — run after regions are annotated and cell types assigned (recipe 3). Within each cell type, compares every gene's expression across the annotated regions with the Kruskal-Wallis test and BH-adjusts across genes; results land in `uns[key_added]`. The plot draws a gene × region mean-expression heatmap (per-gene z-scored) for one cell type — leave `cell_type` blank to auto-pick the most differential one. Uses the normalized/log `.X` from recipe 1, not raw counts.

params.name: cell_type_key
params.tooltip: cell-type column; one Kruskal-Wallis test set per level

params.name: region_key
params.tooltip: annotated region column; the groups each gene is compared across

params.name: cell_type
params.tooltip: which cell type's heatmap to plot (blank = the most differential one)

params.name: key_added
params.tooltip: uns key to store the per-cell-type results under
